$schema_note: Each source is a Source object (see lib/types.ts). 'snippet' is a short excerpt (1–3 sentences) that contains the factual claim we care about. Keep snippets verbatim-ish; they will be shown in the UI and passed verbatim to the LLM judge.

sources.title: Mariana snailfish — Wikipedia (2018 snapshot)
sources.snippet: Pseudoliparis swirei was described from specimens collected in the Mariana Trench at depths of 7,000–8,178 m, making it the deepest-living fish ever recorded.

sources.title: NOAA Ocean Exploration: Life in the Hadal Zone
sources.publisher: NOAA Office of Ocean Exploration and Research
sources.snippet: The Mariana snailfish holds the current depth record for fish, observed at 8,178 meters in 2014 by a joint University of Hawaii / University of Aberdeen expedition.

sources.title: Deepest fish ever filmed at 8,336 m — University of Western Australia
sources.snippet: Scientists from the Minderoo-UWA Deep Sea Research Centre and Tokyo University of Marine Science and Technology have filmed a snailfish at 8,336 m in the Izu-Ogasawara Trench — the deepest fish ever recorded.

sources.title: Snailfish breaks depth record in trench off Japan
sources.snippet: Footage from an autonomous lander in the Izu-Ogasawara Trench shows a juvenile Pseudoliparis at 8,336 metres, 158 m deeper than the previous record-holder.

sources.title: Intro to Oceanography, 5th ed. (chapter excerpt)
sources.snippet: To date, hydrothermal venting has not been directly observed on the ultraslow Gakkel Ridge beneath the Arctic ice pack.

sources.title: First visual observation of vents on the Gakkel Ridge
sources.snippet: Camera-lander imagery from the 2007 AGAVE expedition confirmed active black-smoker venting on the Gakkel Ridge axis at 85°N.
